seo.title: Turning Players Into Heroes — A Who's to Say Media Team Case Study
seo.description: How young adult creators on the Who's to Say Media Team used photography, design, and storytelling to turn Point Pleasant Panthers Challenger Baseball players into trading-card heroes — and gave their families a keepsake to treasure.

listing.title: Turning Players Into Heroes
listing.summary: Young adult creators photographed, designed, and produced personalized cards for an adaptive baseball team's inaugural season.
listing.label: Media Team Case Study
listing.imageAlt: A finished Challenger Baseball hero trading card

hero.eyebrow: A Who's to Say Media Team case study
hero.title: Turning players into heroes.
hero.lead: Young adult creators used photography, design, and storytelling to turn adaptive baseball players into trading-card heroes and give their families a keepsake.
hero.imageAlt: A cinematic hero trading card created for a Challenger Baseball player

sections.heading: A team's first season, remembered forever.
paragraphs: The Point Pleasant Panthers Challenger Baseball program played its first season, and the Who's to Say Media Team came to document practices and Opening Day.
The team then turned photographs and player details into personalized trading-card-style keepsakes.
sections.imageAlt: The Point Pleasant Panthers Challenger Baseball team gathered on the field
sections.caption: Point Pleasant Panthers Challenger Baseball · Inaugural season, 2026

sections.heading: Every athlete deserves to be celebrated.
paragraphs: Families often have season photos but rarely a polished, personalized keepsake that captures a player's personality.
The project asked what could happen if the players became the heroes and young creators with disabilities made it happen.

sections.heading: Not just participants. Creators.
sections.lead: Young adults with disabilities stepped into real creative roles across production.

items.title: Photographer
items.body: Captured player portraits and candid team moments.

items.title: Designer
items.body: Worked with templates, layouts, and design layers.

items.title: Editor
items.body: Selected and refined images and backgrounds.

items.title: Creative director
items.body: Reviewed choices so each card felt personal.

sections.heading: A repeatable media-production workflow.
sections.lead: The cards were the deliverable; practical creative experience was the purpose.

items.title: Capture
items.body: Photograph each player and team moments.

items.title: Listen
items.body: Gather the details each player wants represented.

items.title: Organize
items.body: Prepare the material one player at a time.

items.title: Design
items.body: Build each card with images, layers, and layouts.

items.title: Deliver
items.body: Produce a finished card for the player and family.

sections.heading: More than a card. A memory you can hold.

items.imageAlt: Finished Challenger Baseball trading card in a classic layout
items.caption: The personalized card

items.imageAlt: Finished Challenger Baseball hero card in a cinematic layout
items.caption: The hero card

sections.quote: It meant the world to our players to receive their very own personalized baseball card. It was a meaningful and fun gesture to conclude our inaugural season of Challenger Baseball.

sections.heading: One project. Three kinds of belonging.

items.title: The players
items.body: Each athlete received a personalized keepsake.

items.title: The families
items.body: Families received a polished tribute to display and keep.

items.title: The Media Team
items.body: Creators gained production experience while serving their community.

sections.heading: Help bring the Media Team experience to more communities.
sections.body: Support makes room for accessible creative tools, inclusive teams, and real production roles.
